---
title: "A Decision-Centred Modelling Environment"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-banner style="background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0f3460, #16213e); color: white; padding: 4rem 3rem; margin: -1.5rem -1.5rem 3rem -1.5rem; border-radius: 0 0 12px 12px;"}
## A Decision-Centred Modelling Environment {style="color: white; font-size: 2rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}

**Foundations, Architecture, and Application to Industrial Process Heat Decarbonisation**

*Seyed Ahmad Mahmoudi Lahijani — Auckland University of Technology — 2026*

::: {style="margin-top: 1.5rem; display: flex; gap: 1rem; flex-wrap: wrap;"}
[Read the Framework →](manuscript/module-0-orientation.qmd){.btn style="background: white; color: #0f3460; padding: 0.5rem 1.2rem; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none;"}
[View on GitHub →](https://github.com/Ahmad-Mahmoudi-coder/decision-centred-modelling){.btn style="background: transparent; color: white; border: 1px solid white; padding: 0.5rem 1.2rem; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none;"}
:::
:::

::: {.grid style="gap: 2rem; margin-bottom: 3rem;"}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### The Core Finding

> When the Edendale electrification pathway is evaluated 
> against 64 plausible futures, **23 of those futures** 
> produce grid hosting capacity exceedances invisible from 
> within the site boundary alone.
>
> This is the gap this framework fills — made analytically visible.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### The Central Commitment

> *The decision makes the boundary;*  
> *the artefact makes the connection.*

The framework is organised around the decision, not around 
the system. This single reversal changes what can be seen, 
what can be compared, and what constitutes a defensible 
investment recommendation under deep uncertainty.
:::
:::

---

## Quick Navigation {.unnumbered}

| If you want to... | Go to... |
|---|---|
| Start the conceptual argument | [Module 0: Orientation](manuscript/module-0-orientation.qmd) |
| Understand the decision problem | [Module 1: Foundations](manuscript/module-1-foundations.qmd) |
| See the architecture | [Module 3: Architecture](manuscript/module-3-architecture.qmd) |
| Read the NZ context | [Module 5: The New Zealand Context](manuscript/module-5-nz-context.qmd) |
| See what was built | [Module 6: The Proof of Concept](manuscript/module-6-poc.qmd) |
| Read the eleven propositions | [Module 7: §7.6 Eleven Propositions](manuscript/module-7-synthesis.qmd) |
| Browse all key terms | [Glossary](references/glossary.qmd) |

---

## Development Status {.unnumbered}

| Component | Status |
|---|---|
| Front Matter (Context Declaration, Preamble, Navigation Guide) | ✓ Complete |
| Module 0: Orientation | ✓ Complete |
| Module 1: Foundations | ✓ Complete |
| Module 2: Modelling Foundations | ✓ Complete |
| Module 3: Architecture | ✓ Complete |
| Module 4: Domain Translation | ✓ Complete |
| Module 5: The New Zealand Context | ✓ Complete |
| Module 6: The Proof of Concept | ✓ Complete |
| Module 7: Extension and Synthesis | ✓ Complete |
| Figures and visualisations | ○ In progress |
| arXiv preprint | ○ Pending |
